OSChina.title: COPU 名誉主席陆首群与 Linux 基金会执行董事 Jim Zemlin 会晤
OSChina.description: Linux基金会执行董事Jim Zemlin一行于4月16日在北京与中国开源软件推进联盟(COPU)名誉主席陆首群一行友情会晤。双方讨论国内和全球开源、深度信息技术和人工智能发展问题。 Jim介绍由Linux基金会宣布在美国成立的智能体人工智能基金会(Agentic AI Foundation，AAIF)的情况； 陆主席介绍拟于6月25~26日召开《第21届开源中...

OSChina.title: Guava 33.6.0 发布，Google 的 Java 核心工具库
OSChina.description:
Guava 33.6.0 现已发布。Guava 是 Google 的一套核心 Java 库，包括新的集合类型（如 multimap 和 multiset）、图库，以及用于并发、I/O、散列、缓存、基元、字符串等实用工具。它被广泛用于 Google 内部的 Java 项目，同时也被许多其他公司广泛使用。 Maven 
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.google.guava</groupId>

OSChina.title: TinyVue 3.30 正式发布
OSChina.description: 近期，OpenTiny 社区正式发布 TinyVue v3.30.0！这个版本不仅是一次常规的功能迭代，更是 TinyVue 在 “跨端响应式” 上的里程碑式飞跃。

OSChina.title: LinkedIn 数据显示：AI 并非影响就业的罪魁祸首
OSChina.description: 微软旗下职业社交网站领英（LinkedIn）的全球事务与法律事务主管 Blake Lawit 在接受采访时证实，该公司数据显示自 2022 年以来，招聘人数下降了约 20%。 但他反驳了将责任归咎于 AI 的说法。“我们对此进行了研究——因为大家都想知道这个问题的答案：AI 目前是否正在影响就业？我们进行了研究，老实说，我们尚未观察到...

OSChina.title: Claude 背刺用户，国产大模型谁能顶上？
OSChina.description: 2026 年 4 月 14 日，Anthropic 在 Claude Support 帮助页面悄然上线身份验证说明文档 —— 这是主流 AI 应用中首家大规模实施 KYC 的案例。

Changelog.title: Astral has been acquired by OpenAI
Changelog.description: Astral is joining OpenAI, which says a lot about where the center of gravity is moving for developer tools, LiteLLM got hit by a nasty supply-chain attack, and OpenCode blew up as the latest serious open source swing at the coding-agent stack. We've also got Rust doing a very public reality check on

Changelog.title: From Tailnet to platform
Changelog.description: Adam talks with Tailscale co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer David Carney about where Tailscale is headed next: TSIDP, TSNet, multiple tailnets, and Aperture. They get into clickless auth (via TSIDP), TSNet apps, multiple tailnets for isolation and control, and Aperture, Tailscale’s private AI ga

Changelog.title: Big change brings big change
Changelog.description: This week's been wild — Iran bombed AWS data centers to take down Claude, OpenAI dropped GPT-5.4 (and it's seriously good for coding), and living brain cells are literally playing DOOM. We've also got a heartfelt take on what it feels like to be a 10x engineer in the age of AI, plus some cool new to

Changelog.title: Finale & Friends
Changelog.description: Adam and Jerod get into the news, Jerod officially retires from the pod (and Changelog), plus a bonus for our Changelog++ subs!

Changelog.title: Opus 4.5 changed everything
Changelog.description: Burke Holland works on GitHub Copilot by day and codes with his AI agents always. Early January, Burke posted about how Opus 4.5 changed everything. We were all still buzzing from the holiday-season 2x usage bump Claude gave us, and Opus 4.5 felt like a genuine step function in capability. Burke and

Changelog.title: The mythical agent-month
Changelog.description: Wes McKinney on the mythical agent-month, install Peon Ping to employ a Peon today, Andreas Kling explains why Ladybird is adopting Rust, Cloudflare has a new MCP server that's quite efficient, and Elliot Bonneville thinks the only moat left is money.

Changelog.title: Selling SDKs in the era of many Claudes
Changelog.description:
Steve Ruiz joins us for a deep-dive on tldraw (a very good free whiteboard) and the business he's built selling SDKs that help others build very good whiteboards (and more) with tldraw's high-performance web canvas.

Along the way, we discuss the excitement/fear we share about keeping our agents b

Changelog.title: All the Claw things
Changelog.description: Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI, ZeroClaw is "claw done right", MimiClaw runs on a $5 chip, Steve Yegge on managing the AI Vampire, and the day the telnet died.

Changelog.title: Han shot first
Changelog.description: Our ol' friend, Brett Cannon, is back to talk all things Python. But first! Star Wars, Machete Order, Lost, Babylon 5, Game of Thrones, Murderbot, Ted Lasso, Project Hail Mary, David Attenborough, perpetual voice rights, and the AI uncanny valley.

Changelog.title: Building the machine that builds the machine
Changelog.description:
Paul Dix joins us to discuss the InfluxDB co-founder's journey adapting to an agentic world. Paul sent his AI coding agents on various real-world side quests and shares all his findings: what's going to prod, what's not, and why he's (at least for a bit) back to coding by hand.

Update: He's back

The self-evolving paradigm offers a promising path, but its reliance on model consensus to construct pseudo-label

HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Memory-based self-evolution has emerged as a promising paradigm for coding agents. However, existing approaches typically restrict memory utilization to homogeneous task domains, failing to leverage the shared infrastructural foundations, such as runtime environments and programming languages, that
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Kangsan Kim, Minki Kang, Taeil Kim, Yanlai Yang, Mengye Ren, Sung Ju Hwang

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: From P(y|x) to P(y): Investigating Reinforcement Learning in Pre-train Space
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: While reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances LLM reasoning by optimizing the conditional distribution P(y|x), its potential is fundamentally bounded by the base model's existing output distribution. Optimizing the marginal distribution P(y) in the Pre-train Spac
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Yuqiao Tan, Minzheng Wang, Bo Liu, Zichen Liu, Tian Liang, Shizhu He, Jun Zhao, Kang Liu

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Free Geometry: Refining 3D Reconstruction from Longer Versions of Itself
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Feed-forward 3D reconstruction models are efficient but rigid: once trained, they perform inference in a zero-shot manner and cannot adapt to the test scene. As a result, visually plausible reconstructions often contain errors, particularly under occlusions, specularities, and ambiguous cues. To add
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Yuhang Dai, Xingyi Yang

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: TIP: Token Importance in On-Policy Distillation
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: On-policy knowledge distillation (OPD) trains a student on its own rollouts under token-level supervision from a teacher. Not all token positions matter equally, but existing views of token importance are incomplete. We ask a direct question: which tokens carry the most useful learning signal in OPD
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Yuanda Xu, Hejian Sang, Zhengze Zhou, Ran He, Zhipeng Wang, Alborz Geramifard

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: LangFlow: Continuous Diffusion Rivals Discrete in Language Modeling
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Continuous diffusion has been the foundation of high-fidelity, controllable, and few-step generation of many data modalities such as images. However, in language modeling, prior continuous diffusion language models (DLMs) lag behind discrete counterparts due to the sparse data space and the underexp
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Yuxin Chen, Chumeng Liang, Hangke Sui, Ruihan Guo, Chaoran Cheng, Jiaxuan You, Ge Liu

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered AI research agents to perform isolated scientific tasks, automating complex, real-world workflows, such as LLM training, remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we introduce TREX, a multi-agent system that automates the entire LLM training li
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Zerun Ma, Guoqiang Wang, Xinchen Xie, Yicheng Chen, He Du, Bowen Li, Yanan Sun, Wenran Liu, Kai Chen, Yining Li

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: UI-Zoomer: Uncertainty-Driven Adaptive Zoom-In for GUI Grounding
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: GUI grounding, which localizes interface elements from screenshots given natural language queries, remains challenging for small icons and dense layouts. Test-time zoom-in methods improve localization by cropping and re-running inference at higher resolution, but apply cropping uniformly across all
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Fei Tang, Bofan Chen, Zhengxi Lu, Tongbo Chen, Songqin Nong, Tao Jiang, Wenhao Xu, Weiming Lu, Jun Xiao, Yueting Zhuang, Yongliang Shen

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: MERRIN: A Benchmark for Multimodal Evidence Retrieval and Reasoning in Noisy Web Environments
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Motivated by the underspecified, multi-hop nature of search queries and the multimodal, heterogeneous, and often conflicting nature of real-world web results, we introduce MERRIN (Multimodal Evidence Retrieval and Reasoning in Noisy Web Environments), a human-annotated benchmark for evaluating searc

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: UI-Copilot: Advancing Long-Horizon GUI Automation via Tool-Integrated Policy Optimization
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: MLLM-based GUI agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in complex user interface interaction tasks. HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Geometric Context Transformer for Streaming 3D Reconstruction
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Streaming 3D reconstruction aims to recover 3D information, such as camera poses and point clouds, from a video stream, which necessitates geometric accuracy, temporal   consistency, and computational efficiency. Motivated by the principles of Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM), we introdu
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Lin-Zhuo Chen, Jian Gao, Yihang Chen, Ka Leong Cheng, Yipengjing Sun, Liangxiao Hu, Nan Xue, Xing Zhu, Yujun Shen, Yao Yao, Yinghao Xu

HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Existing segmentation models based on multimodal large language models (MLLMs), such as LISA, often struggle with novel or emerging entities due to their inability to incorporate up-to-date knowledge. To address this challenge, we introduce the Novel Emerging Segmentation Task (NEST), which focuses
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Song Tang, Guangquan Jie, Henghui Ding, Yu-Gang Jiang

HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Language Model (LM) agents are increasingly used in complex open-ended decision-making tasks, from AI coding to physical AI. A core requirement in these settings is the ability to both explore the problem space and exploit acquired knowledge effectively. However, systematically distinguishing and qu
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Jaden Park, Jungtaek Kim, Jongwon Jeong, Robert D. Nowak, Kangwook Lee, Yong Jae Lee

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: InfiniteScienceGym: An Unbounded, Procedurally-Generated Benchmark for Scientific Analysis
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Large language models are emerging as scientific assistants, but evaluating their ability to reason from empirical data remains challenging. LWN.title: Rust 1.95.0 released
LWN.description:
Version
1.95.0 of the Rust language has been released. Changes include the
addition of a cfg_select!
macro, the capability to use if let guards to allow conditionals based on pattern
matching, and many newly stabilized APIs. See the release
notes for a full list of changes.
LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: Forgejo 15.0 released
LWN.description:
Version
15.0 of the Forgejo
code-collaboration platform has been released. Changes include
repository-specific access tokens, a number of improvements to Forgejo
Actions, user-interface enhancements, and more. Forgejo 15.0 is
considered a long-term-support (LTS) release, and will be supported
throug
LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: [$] The first half of the 7.1 merge window
LWN.description:
The 7.1 merge window opened on April 12 with the release

pulled into the mainline repository for the next release.  This merge
window is thus just getting started, but there has still been a fair amount
of interesting work moving i

include improvements in the Merkuro

the ability to add keyboard shortcuts in the Dolphin file manager

LWN.title: [$] LWN.net Weekly Edition for April 16, 2026
LWN.description:
Inside this week's LWN.net Weekly Edition:
        
 Front: LLM security reports; OpenWrt One build system; Vim forks; removing read-only THPs; 7.0 statistics; MusicBrainz Picard.
            
 Briefs: OpenSSL 4.0.0; Relicensing; Servo; Zig 0.16.0; Quotes; ...
            
 Announcements: Newsletter
LWN.author: corbet

LWN.title: FSF clarifies its stance on AGPLv3 additional terms
LWN.description:
OnlyOffice CEO Lev Bannov has recently
claimed that the Euro-Office fork of the
OnlyOffice suite violates the GNU Affero General Public License
version 3 (AGPLv3). Krzysztof Siewicz of the Free Software
Foundation (FSF) has published
an article on the FSF's position on adding terms to the AGPLv3. In
LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: [$] Forking Vim to avoid LLM-generated code
LWN.description:
Many people dislike the proliferation of Large Language Models (LLMs) in recent
years, and so make an understandable attempt to avoid them.
That may not be possible in general, but there are two new forks of
Vim that seek to provide an editing
environment with no LLM-generated code. EVi focuses on b
LWN.author: daroc

LWN.description: Security updates have been issued by AlmaLinux (capstone, cockpit, firefox, git-lfs, golang-github-openprinting-ipp-usb, kea, kernel, nghttp2, nodejs24, openexr, perl-XML-Parser, rsync, squid, and vim), Debian (imagemagick, systemd, and thunderbird), Slackware (libexif and xorg), SUSE (bind, clamav,
LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: Zig 0.16.0 released
LWN.description:
The Zig project has announced version
0.16.0 of the Zig programming language.
This release features 8 months of work: changes
from 244 different contributors, spread among
1183 commits.
Perhaps most notably, this release debuts I/O
as an Interface, but don't sleep on the Language
Changes or enhancem
LWN.author: jzb
